# Franklin 1952 — Photo 51

Rosalind Franklin's B-form DNA diffraction pattern, taken May 1952 at King's
College London. The cross-shaped reflection at the center is the diagnostic
signature of a helix. Pitch ~34 Å, diameter ~20 Å.

## Key observations

- B-form is hydrated, A-form is dehydrated.
- Meridional reflection at 3.4 Å gives base stacking distance.
- The 10:1 ratio between pitch and rise implies 10 bases per turn.
